atDirectives.name: @tailwind
atDirectives.description: Use the @tailwind directive to insert Tailwind's `base`, `components`, `utilities` and `screens` styles into your CSS.

atDirectives.name: @apply
atDirectives.description: Use @apply to inline any existing utility classes into your own custom CSS.

atDirectives.name: @layer
atDirectives.description: Use @layer to tell Tailwind which 'bucket' a set of custom styles belong to. Valid layers are base, components, and utilities.

atDirectives.name: @config
atDirectives.description: Use @config to specify which config file Tailwind should use when compiling that CSS file.

atDirectives.name: @screen
atDirectives.description: Use @screen to create media queries that reference your breakpoints by name instead of duplicating their values.

atDirectives.name: @variants
atDirectives.description: Use @variants to generate responsive, hover, focus, active, and other variants of your own utilities.

pseudoClasses.name: :host-context
pseudoClasses.description: Angular component host context selector for theming

pseudoClasses.name: ::ng-deep
pseudoClasses.description: Angular deep selector to pierce component encapsulation (deprecated but still used)
